chapter.label: Tale 158
chapter.title: An Ass In The Service Of The Monastery Called Mardes

paragraphs.text: There is a mountain by the Dead Sea called Mardes and it is very high. There are anchorites living in that mountain. They have a garden about six miles away from where they live, near the edge of the Sea, almost on its banks. One of the anchorites is stationed there to tend the garden. At whatever hour the anchorites wish to send to the garden for vegetables, they put a pack-saddle on the ass and say to it: ‘Go to the one who tends the garden and bring us some vegetables’. It goes of alone to find the gardener; when it stands before the doer, it knocks with its head. The gardener loads it up with vegetables and sends it away. You can see the ass returning alone cach time, but it only serves those elders; it supplies the needs of nobody else.
